name: Subworkflow
summary: Delegates processing to another FlowDSL workflow and returns the result.
description:
# Subworkflow

Invokes a separate FlowDSL workflow by reference, passing the input packet as the entrypoint message. The child workflow executes to completion and returns its terminal output back to the parent flow.

## Use cases
- Compose large pipelines from reusable sub-pipelines
- Encapsulate complex processing behind a single node
- Call shared workflows maintained by other teams
- Build hierarchical flow architectures

## Execution model
- **Synchronous**: waits for the child workflow to complete before emitting output
- **Input mapping**: the input packet is passed as the child flow's entrypoint message
- **Output mapping**: the child flow's terminal node output becomes this node's output
- **Error propagation**: if the child workflow fails, the error propagates to the parent edge's retry policy

## Notes
- `workflowRef` can be a local `#/flows/child_flow` reference or a remote URL
- Timeout inherited from parent node's `execution.timeoutMs` or the child flow's own limits
- Nested subworkflows are supported (depth limit enforced by runtime)

inputs.name: Input
inputs.description: Packet passed as the entrypoint message of the child workflow.

schema.description: Dynamic schema — must match the child workflow's entrypoint expectation.

outputs.name: Output
outputs.description: Terminal output from the child workflow.

schema.description: Dynamic schema — determined by the child workflow's terminal node output.

workflowRef.title: Workflow Reference
workflowRef.description: Reference to the child workflow. Local: #/flows/my_flow. Remote: https://example.com/flow.flowdsl.json#/flows/main.

inputMapping.title: Input Mapping
inputMapping.description: Optional field mapping from parent packet to child entrypoint. Omit to pass the full packet.

outputMapping.title: Output Mapping
outputMapping.description: Optional field mapping from child terminal output to parent packet. Omit to return the full output.

timeoutMs.title: Timeout (ms)
timeoutMs.description: Maximum time to wait for the child workflow to complete. 0 = inherit from execution config.

failOnChildError.title: Fail on Child Error
failOnChildError.description: If true, child workflow errors propagate as node failure. If false, errors are returned in the output packet.
